title: Fiery Steamed Jamaican Fish Tagine
description: Pungent and springy Jamaican fish tagine featuring cinnamon, brine-wet, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors. Perfect for a sick-day comfort dish.
instructions: Rinse the grouper fillets under cold water, pat them dry with paper towels, and season with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground cinnamon, allspice, and cayenne pepper. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fresh grouper fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they develop a golden brown crust.
Transfer the Dutch oven to a stovetop-safe steamer basket. Pour in the water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook the grouper for 8-10 minutes, or until it flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the grouper from the steamer basket and place it on a serving platter.
Strain the cooking liquid and discard the solids. Spoon the sauce over the grouper fillets.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
Serve with steamed vegetables or rice for a complete meal.
